talk_metadata.talk_title: Overview of RAS inhibitor landscape and resistance mechanisms
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "the responses to RAS inhibitors are limited in terms of the response rate as well as the duration of response"
Speaker said: "we are dealing with issues of primary resistance as well as acquired resistance"
Speaker said: "we see that DNA alterations are found in about 50% of patients, but also non-genetic alterations are seen in another 50%"
Speaker said: "I think my bias is always to do concurrent, to not let the train leave the station and then try to catch it after it's left"

vulnerability_identified.mechanistic_blindspot: Non-genetic transcriptional mechanisms of resistance not fully addressed
vulnerability_identified.unexplained_resistance_quote: Speaker said: "we see that DNA alterations are found in about 50% of patients, but also non-genetic alterations are seen in another 50%"

vulnerability_identified.mechanistic_blindspot: Lack of reactivation of RAS alleles leading to alternative effector mutations
vulnerability_identified.unexplained_resistance_quote: Speaker said: "for the pan‑RAS activity, we see no reactivation of RAS, and instead mutations that prevent effector inhibitor binding"

trial_dilution_risk.flawed_enrollment_criteria: Enrolling all‑comers without biomarker stratification for co‑occurring MAPK pathway alterations
trial_dilution_risk.statistical_concern: Response rates ~40% with median duration <10 months may reflect underpowered subgroup analyses

competitive_moat_weakness.ip_vulnerability_note: Speaker highlighted that resistance emerges via secondary KRAS mutations reactivating the pathway, suggesting the covalent scaffold can be bypassed
competitive_moat_weakness.clinical_strategy_weakness: Broad enrollment dilutes signal; lack of biomarker‑guided combination strategies

cognitive_dissonance: Speaker showed that ERK signaling is suppressed by RAS inhibitor in on‑treatment biopsies, then concluded that concurrent ERK inhibition may be unnecessary despite acknowledging ERK reactivation as a common resistance mechanism

key_data_gaps: No overall survival data for most early‑phase RAS inhibitor studies
Limited longitudinal ctDNA tracking of resistance

crispro_opportunity.description: Non‑genetic transcriptional resistance (TEAD, MYC, ERK) could be targeted by CrisPRO‑derived transcriptional signatures to identify patients who may benefit from orthogonal combinations.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "we identified TEAD, MYC and ERK transcription factor‑based mechanisms of resistance"
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: Use CrisPRO to model the shared 300‑gene core network and predict combinatorial targets that disrupt the transcriptional resistance program.

crispro_opportunity.description: Absence of pre‑treatment MAPK pathway activation biomarkers limits patient selection for G12C inhibitors.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "we see that DNA alterations are found in about 50% of patients, but also non‑genetic alterations are seen in another 50%"
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: Apply CrisPRO to integrate genomic and transcriptomic data to generate a composite MAPK activation score for enrollment.

cited_competitors.name: Daraxasib
cited_competitors.context: Mentioned as a wild‑type and mutant RAS inhibitor that defied pre‑clinical expectations

cited_competitors.name: MRTX1133
cited_competitors.context: Cited as a KRAS G12D inhibitor in preclinical models

unresolved_questions: What are the precise non‑genetic mechanisms driving resistance in the 50% of patients without DNA alterations?
Can dynamic ctDNA monitoring improve early detection of emerging MAPK reactivation?

talk_metadata.talk_title: BMAL2 as a KRAS‑linked hypoxia driver in pancreatic cancer
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "BMAL2 was the top regulatory protein associated with KRAS activity"
Speaker said: "BMAL2 knockout reduces proliferation and EMT, while overexpression drives mesenchymal phenotype"
Speaker said: "BMAL2 knockout cells remain sensitive to RAS inhibitors, suggesting distinct resistance mechanisms"

vulnerability_identified.mechanistic_blindspot: BMAL2 upregulation sustains HIF‑2α signaling and EMT despite KRAS inhibition
vulnerability_identified.unexplained_resistance_quote: Speaker said: "we haven't checked if BMAL2 is affected in resistant tumors; single‑cell data suggest it goes up again in the resistant group"

competitive_moat_weakness.ip_vulnerability_note: Speaker disclosed that BMAL2 shares homology with HIF‑1β/2β, indicating potential redundancy with HIF pathways that could be bypassed
competitive_moat_weakness.clinical_strategy_weakness: No current clinical agents directly target BMAL2; reliance on indirect KRAS inhibition may leave this pathway unchecked

cognitive_dissonance: Speaker showed that BMAL2 knockout reduces EMT and proliferation, yet predicted that combining BMAL2 inhibition with KRAS inhibitors would be additive without providing pre‑clinical combination data

key_data_gaps: Lack of in vivo combination studies with KRAS inhibitors
No assessment of BMAL2 status in patient resistance biopsies

crispro_opportunity.description: CrisPRO can model BMAL2‑driven transcriptional networks to identify downstream druggable nodes (e.g., glycolysis enzymes) for combination with KRAS inhibitors.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "metabolomics showed glycolysis as top differential pathway in BMAL2 knockout under hypoxia"
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: Predict metabolic vulnerabilities created by BMAL2 loss and prioritize agents that target glycolysis in KRAS‑inhibited tumors.

crispro_opportunity.description: Develop a BMAL2 activity signature to stratify patients who may benefit from KRAS plus hypoxia‑targeted therapy.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "BMAL2 activity correlates with KRAS activity across pancreatic, lung and colorectal cancers"
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: Use CrisPRO to integrate BMAL2 regulon with clinical outcomes to define a predictive biomarker.

cited_competitors.name: Revolution Medicines RNC7977
cited_competitors.context: Used as KRAS inhibitor to validate BMAL2 down‑regulation

unresolved_questions: Does BMAL2 up‑regulation directly cause resistance to KRAS inhibitors in patients?
What is the functional relationship between BMAL2 and HIF‑2α in resistant tumors?

talk_metadata.talk_title: RTK rewiring and ADC internalization as resistance mechanisms
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "RTK activity and expression drive resistance to RAS inhibition"
Speaker said: "combination of EGFR inhibition with KRAS inhibitor is approved for colorectal cancer"
Speaker said: "HER2 internalization can be enhanced by irreversible TKIs to improve ADC efficacy"
Speaker said: "we observed that RAS inhibitor increases HER2 and ER3 expression over time"

vulnerability_identified.mechanistic_blindspot: Low HER2 expression and poor internalization in pancreatic tumor cells
vulnerability_identified.unexplained_resistance_quote: Speaker said: "T‑DxD shows response in many cancers but not in pancreatic cancer despite similar HER2 levels"

trial_dilution_risk.trial_name_or_nct: Combination of KRAS inhibitor with EGFR inhibitor (generic)
trial_dilution_risk.target_biology: Concurrent blockade of KRAS and EGFR signaling
trial_dilution_risk.flawed_enrollment_criteria: Enrolling all KRAS‑mutant patients regardless of EGFR dependency or allele (G12 vs Q61)
trial_dilution_risk.missing_biomarker: Baseline EGFR dependency or KRAS allele‑specific sensitivity

competitive_moat_weakness.ip_vulnerability_note: Speaker revealed that internalization dynamics, not just target expression, limit efficacy, suggesting the scaffold can be circumvented by modulating trafficking
competitive_moat_weakness.clinical_strategy_weakness: Reliance on HER2 expression without ensuring adequate receptor internalization

cognitive_dissonance: Speaker demonstrated that HER2 expression increases after KRAS inhibition but concluded that HER2 ADCs are ineffective in pancreatic cancer, ignoring the potential to exploit the induced expression for better internalization

key_data_gaps: Lack of in vivo validation of HER2 internalization‑enhancing combos
No clinical data linking RTK up‑regulation to patient outcomes

crispro_opportunity.description: CrisPRO can predict RTK internalization kinetics from transcriptomic signatures to select ADCs with optimal payload delivery in KRAS‑inhibited tumors.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "we can see that RAS inhibitor increases the expression of receptors, but not all of them. EGFR stays steady, HER2 and ER3 are dynamically different"
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: Model receptor trafficking networks to identify agents that boost internalization, enabling superior ADC design.

crispro_opportunity.description: Identify small‑molecule TKIs that promote HER2/ER3 internalization as partners for HER2‑ADC therapy in pancreatic cancer.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "irreversible TKI that displays HSP90 allows HER2 internalization"
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: Use CrisPRO to simulate kinase inhibition effects on RTK dimerization and endocytic pathways.

cited_competitors.name: T‑DxD (HER2 ADC)
cited_competitors.context: Discussed as ineffective in pancreatic cancer despite HER2 expression

cited_competitors.name: Afatinib
cited_competitors.context: Mentioned as toxic EGFR inhibitor used in combination strategies

unresolved_questions: Can sequential EGFR inhibition followed by ADC delivery achieve comparable efficacy with reduced toxicity?
What are the stromal contributions to RTK expression dynamics after KRAS inhibition?

talk_metadata.talk_title: Mechanisms of resistance to RAS inhibition in pancreatic cancer
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "near doubling of overall survival, hazard ratio of 0.4"
Speaker said: "about 60% of patients we identified a putative mechanism of resistance in ctDNA"
Speaker said: "two‑thirds of those patients had mutant KRAS amplification"
Speaker said: "we see suppressed MAPK signaling at resistance across subtypes"

vulnerability_identified.mechanistic_blindspot: Mutant KRAS amplification as a genetic bypass; non‑genetic mechanisms remain undefined
vulnerability_identified.unexplained_resistance_quote: Speaker said: "in about 40% of patients we weren't able to identify a mechanism of resistance"
vulnerability_identified.ghost_responder_admission: Some patients achieve deep, durable responses while others progress quickly

trial_dilution_risk.target_biology: Pan‑RAS inhibition in second‑line pancreatic cancer
trial_dilution_risk.flawed_enrollment_criteria: Broad enrollment without stratifying by KRAS allele type (G12D vs Q61) or baseline MAPK activity
trial_dilution_risk.missing_biomarker: Baseline KRAS allele‑specific dependency and MAPK pathway activation score
trial_dilution_risk.static_vs_dynamic_failure: No serial ctDNA or on‑treatment biopsies for all patients
trial_dilution_risk.statistical_concern: Potential dilution of OS benefit due to heterogeneous responder population

competitive_moat_weakness.ip_vulnerability_note: Speaker noted that wild‑type KRAS inhibition may limit therapeutic window and drive selection for mutant allele amplification
competitive_moat_weakness.clinical_strategy_weakness: Concurrent use of chemotherapy may be needed but not yet optimized

cognitive_dissonance: Speaker presented suppressed MAPK signaling in resistant tumors yet concluded that MAPK pathway remains a viable target for combination without providing functional validation

key_data_gaps: Mechanistic basis for the 40% ctDNA‑negative resistance cases
Functional validation of KRAS amplification as driver of resistance

crispro_opportunity.description: Use CrisPRO to model the impact of KRAS amplification on downstream signaling and predict combination partners that suppress the amplified output.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "two‑thirds of those patients had mutant KRAS amplification"
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: Simulate dosage‑dependent KRAS signaling to identify optimal inhibitor concentrations or orthogonal targets (e.g., PRMT5).

crispro_opportunity.description: Develop a composite biomarker (KRAS allele, MAPK activity, mucin‑secretory program) to select patients for KRAS inhibitor versus combination therapy.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "we see a conserved secretory epithelial program across subtypes at resistance"
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: Integrate multi‑omics to generate a predictive score for response vs resistance.

cited_competitors.name: MRTX1133 (KRAS G12D inhibitor)
cited_competitors.context: Cited as preclinical agent showing durable responses in mouse models

cited_competitors.name: Revolution Medicines
cited_competitors.context: Provided duraxinrasib and supported the clinical trial

unresolved_questions: What non‑genetic pathways sustain tumor growth despite suppressed MAPK signaling?
Can early ctDNA monitoring of KRAS amplification predict imminent clinical progression?
